title: How can I use Monday to take and organize meeting notes?
excerpt: Hi, I’d like to set up a board where I can take meeting notes, that include the participants, agenda, links, background info, and then minutes, and ideally tasks. How can I use Monday to do that for a variety of projects…

posts.author: tkatelman (Tracy Katelman)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, I’d like to set up a board where I can take meeting notes, that include the participants, agenda, links, background info, and then minutes, and ideally tasks. How can I use Monday to do that for a variety of projects? I was thinking to set up a folder where I would save these for each meeting related to a similar project, or something like that…

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @tkatelman - have you looked into Monday Docs? This may be better suited and be used for logging ongoing meeting notes or an individual doc per meeting. There are doc templates as well and have some for meeting minutes.

Hope this helps!
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: tkatelman (Tracy Katelman)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Mark. Yes, I’m using Monday Docs in other places. That Meeting Notes template could work. Is there a way to then link my new Doc (for each meeting) to other places in Monday? I assume through the Files field?

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @tkatelman - yes, you can use a Files column and link it directly to your docs.

posts.author: mauriziodelmonte (maurizio)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, please be aware that docs in monday are not searchable: Search results include contents of Monday Docs - #15 by involuntary_admin

posts.author: Benkazinik (Ben Kazinik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Want to get better at taking notes? Check out our best strategies on note taking.
Also, check out monday’s awesome meeting notes template.
